# embertoast

> Headless-first toast/notification primitive for React. Zero runtime dependencies.
> Call `toast()` from anywhere (even outside React); mount one `<Toaster/>` to render.
> Package: `@embertoast/react` (React binding) · `@embertoast/core` (framework-agnostic store).

## Install

```
npm i @embertoast/react      # or: pnpm add / yarn add / bun add
```

## Setup — do this ONCE, near the app root (e.g. the root layout)

```tsx
import { Toaster } from "@embertoast/react";
import "@embertoast/react/styles.css"; // omit only if you render your own markup

// Mount a single <Toaster/>. It portals to <body>, so it's never trapped by a
// transformed/blurred ancestor; position is viewport-relative.
<Toaster position="bottom-right" theme="system" />
```

## Fire toasts — from anywhere (components, event handlers, non-React modules)

```ts
import { toast } from "@embertoast/react";

toast("Saved");
toast.success("Profile updated");
toast.error("Upload failed");
toast.info("3 new comments");
toast.warning("Low on storage");
toast.loading("Working…");          // persistent until updated/dismissed
```

## Rich toast (all options are optional)

```ts
toast.success("Task has been updated with the recent changes.", {
  title: "Changes saved",            // bold heading; the first arg becomes the body
  timestamp: Date.now(),             // number = epoch ms (auto-formatted) | string (verbatim)
  duration: 4000,                    // ms; Infinity = stays open
  timerBar: true,                    // depleting countdown bar at the bottom edge
  footer: "Closes in 4s. Hover to stop.",
  actions: [                         // OPTIONAL CTAs — omit for no buttons
    { label: "Undo", variant: "button", onClick: undo },   // filled button
    { label: "Details", onClick: openDetails },            // underlined link (default)
  ],
});
```

## Promise — one toast morphs loading → success/error in place

```ts
toast.promise(saveFile(file), {
  loading: "Saving…",
  success: (res) => `Saved ${res.name}`,
  error: (err) => `Failed: ${err.message}`,
});
```

## Upload / determinate progress

```ts
const id = toast.info("0%", { title: "Uploading…", duration: Infinity, progress: 0 });
toast.update(id, { progress: 0.5, message: "50%" });               // patch as it advances
toast.success("Done", { id, progress: undefined, timerBar: true }); // clear bar + morph to success
```

## Control

```ts
toast.dismiss(id);          // dismiss one
toast.dismiss();            // dismiss all
toast.update(id, patch);    // patch in place
```

## `<Toaster/>` props

`position` ("top|bottom-left|center|right") · `theme` ("light|dark|system") ·
`visibleToasts` (number, rest queue) · `closeButton` (boolean) · `expand` (boolean) ·
`gap` / `offset` (px) · `container` (`HTMLElement | false` — defaults to `document.body`).

## Headless (bring your own UI)

Skip the stylesheet, subscribe with `useToasts(): Toast[]`, and render your own markup.
`@embertoast/core` holds the state; the renderer never owns it.

## Rules for code generation (read these)

- Mount EXACTLY ONE `<Toaster/>` for the whole app (root layout/_app), not per component.
- `toast(...)` is a function, not a hook — import and call it anywhere, including outside React.
- Import `@embertoast/react/styles.css` once for the styled default (or go headless).
- The package is `@embertoast/react` (not "embertoast"); the core is `@embertoast/core`.
- Do not add a second toast library alongside it.

Full docs: https://embertoast.dev
